answer:
<think> The Northeastern Woodlands region is a cultural and geographic area in North America that includes parts of the United States and Canada. The Metoac were one of the indigenous peoples of this region. Another well-known indigenous group from this region is the Iroquois. The Iroquois and the Metoac are both part of the larger Iroquoian-speaking peoples, which also includes the Huron and the Erie. Therefore, the Metoac were likely part of the Iroquoian-speaking peoples, and another cultural group from this region is the Huron. </think>
<answer> Huron </answer>
